purpose: Bootstrap module for lightweight local data CLI tools; installs DuckDB CLI from GitHub releases and sqlite-utils/csvkit via pipx, then records all three in the manifest.
invariants: Heavy DS/ML libraries (pandas, numpy, torch) are intentionally excluded — those are project-local only
DuckDB is installed as a standalone binary into ~/tools/bin, not via pip
data_install is idempotent: each helper checks for presence before acting
data_record_manifest only adds a tool to the manifest if its binary is found on PATH at record time
gotchas: DUCKDB_SHA256 is intentionally left empty — the operator must pin it after testing a new release; an empty value triggers a warning but does not block installation
The pip `duckdb` package is the Python library, not the CLI — they are different artifacts from the same project
csvkit exposes multiple binaries (csvlook, csvcut, csvstat, in2csv); manifest_add uses csvlook as the canonical detection binary

key_functions.name: data_duckdb
key_functions.summary: Downloads and installs the DuckDB standalone CLI binary from GitHub releases, with SHA256 verification

key_functions.name: data_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts duckdb, sqlite-utils, and csvkit into manifest/tools.json if their binaries are present
